title: polygonum aviculare subsp. induratum
intro: Polygonum aviculare subsp. induratum, also known as the creeping knotweed or hard knotweed, is a subspecies of the Polygonum aviculare plant, a flowering perennial herb native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The herb can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
